title: Mild to Moderate Allergic Reaction
immediate_steps: Remove the allergen if known (e.g., wash off a plant sap, remove a stinger)
Apply a cool compress or calamine lotion to itchy skin
Monitor the person closely for 30-60 minutes in case symptoms worsen
avoid: Do not ignore symptoms as they can progress
Do not apply heat to rashes

title: Anaphylaxis (Severe Allergic Reaction)
symptoms: Swelling of the throat, lips, tongue, or face
Tightness in the chest or throat
immediate_steps: Ask if they have an epinephrine auto-injector (EpiPen) and help them inject it into the outer mid-thigh
Have the person lie down on their back. If breathing is difficult, let them sit up. If they feel faint, elevate their legs.
If they vomit, turn them on their side
Loosen tight clothing and keep them warm
If symptoms don't improve after 5-15 minutes, administer a second EpiPen if available
Begin CPR if they become unresponsive and stop breathing
avoid: Do not wait to see if symptoms improve before using an EpiPen or calling 911
Do not give them anything to eat or drink
Do not place a pillow under their head if they are having trouble breathing
